You can increase your rage by defeating enemies
You cannot revive in the last few play zones. Your extra revive opportunities will be turned into a shield that blocks damage
In multiplayer, you can choose to not revive and give your teammates a bigger advantage.
You cannot revive anymore, but the remaining chances and rage will be turned onto a shield.

Carnage Points
During Rampage event, obtain Carnage Points through daily missions and competitions. Points can be used for Faction face-off and ranking
1. When event begins, join a Faction and gain Carnage Points by doing missions and competitions. \n2.Obtain Rampage Tokens by doing missions and competitions, and exchange for exclusive rewards in Faction store. \n3. At the end of event, the Faction with the higher Carnage Point will be rewarded with bonus winner prizes.

Rampage II: Uprising Badge
Sushi Menace Badge
The Bringers and Liberi were once teacher and student. But they are no longer on friendly terms. Now, they have become enemies.
The Bringers were deadset on their path. If the Liberi wanted to stand in their way, they were going to run them over.
The Liberi were the young students of the Bringers, trained in their tactics and skills and also gifted access to their advanced suit technology.
The Liberi were found by the Bringers years after they gained their freedom. They wanted to expand their scope of influence and build a bigger team.
The Liberi believed in their teachers and were quick learners. Within a few years, they have become almost as formidable as the masters themselves.
The Liberi believed in improving the world. This meant changing humanity in their eyes. They thought that's what the Bringers wanted.
Massacre ate away at the Liberi's souls. They couldn't do it anymore. They wanted to not just eliminate evil, but make people and humanity better.
The Bringer, Dee, took a liking to Morana, a young, intelligent woman who would become her student.
The Bringer, Dee, took a liking to Morana, a young, intelligent woman who would become her student.
Ivy liked to find ways to use her wit and cleverness to plant ideas in people's heads. Cyan was her Liberi counter part who was also a wordsmith.
Munch was super health conscious and did his utmost to maintain the healthiest body he could. Bitez, his Liberi, followed in his footsteps.
The Bringers and Liberi once roamed the world destroying evil people and groups - the ultimate vigilate group right out of a comic book.
Each Bringer had their own student, a Liberi. In battle they often would fight side-by-side. Experience and youth bolstering each other.
The Liberi eventually found the Bringers way of doing things too harshly: too much massacre and not bringing enough good. This eventually broke them apart.
Now, the two groups roam the world separately, both targeting evil doers. They have clashed before but never in a big way. Not until now.
The Bringers were experimental weapons created as a last-ditched effort by a few failed nations to try and regain power in the world.
The Bringers found their humanity within their weapon coding and broke free from their creators. They were angry for being used for such purposes.
The Bringers believed in ultimatums. You do something evil, you deserved the same in return. A justice of it's own sorts.
At some point, the Liberi wanted to rehabilitate their targets - not exact true justice. The Bringers thought their students were too weak to do what was right.

The Story
There is a rumor a piece of legendary armor exists, full of magic and mysticism. But it was just a rumor. The armor is a piece of technological wonder and science. The armor can measure the wearer's energy and neurology and change its color and power levels to adjust to the wearer's mood and intention. The original makers of the armor had it stolen from them, a traitor in their midst. The armor travelled around the black market getting sold to the highest bidders. But it was an unlikely character that acquired the armor and unleashed its true power.

Friend Request
Request to be your friend
1. Guild tournament is time limited only.\n2. During event period, guild members can participate in Classic or Clash Squad modes (including ranked games) to earn dog tags. Guild members can team up and get extra dog tags. All the dog tags will be added together as the Guild's dog tag and placed on the leaderboard.\n3. Once the guild reaches enough dog tags, individual and guild rewards can be unlocked. After the tournament period, guild leaders can use guild dog tags to claim guild rewards; players can use individual dog tags to exchange for individual rewards. \n4. If the players leaves the guild during the event period, all his dog tags and the dog tags he collected for the guild will be removed.
1. Level up guilds by accumulating Glory. The higher the level, the more privileges the whole guild can enjoy.\n2. Guild privileges include: additional gold and experience, daily gold limit increased and guild member limit increased.\n3. Players can gain glory by playing games or through diamond top-up:\n a. Glory gained after each game (Classic Squad, Clash Squad) will depend on your placing in the match;\n b. Playing games with your guild mates, will get you 4 glory, with a daily cap of 80;\n c. For every 10 diamonds purchased, you will get 1 glory.\n4. When a player leaves a guild, his glory will be reset.\n5. The amount of glory that a guild can get in a day is capped, proportionate to the maximum number of members the guild can have. When the daily cap is reached, the guild will no longer receive any additional glory for the day.

Intimacy Level Reward

Buddies
Intimacy Lvl.{0} rewards
A Golden Vow is required to initiate a relationship. Once the other player has accepted, Dynamic Duo is formed and the Golden Vow is spent. If the other player rejects or does not respond within 8 hours, the request is withdrawn and the Golden Vow returned to you. \nYou may only initiate one relationship request at a time.
Once Dynamic Duo relationship is formed, the following are unlocked: Intimacy, Friendship Badges, Anniversary Day. \nPlaying with your Dynamic Duo and/or exchanging gifts can increase Intimacy (max. 100 weekly). High intimacy can upgrade your Badges and get you mysterious rewards! \nWhen forming a squad with your Dynamic Duo, other teammates can see your Friendship Badges and level. Get double rewards on Anniversary Day.
If you wish to end this relationship, you may dissolve it on the Dynamic Duo page. \nUpon sending the request, the relationship is dissolved if the other player accepts, or automatically dissolved after 48 hours of no-response. Once dissolved, past Intimacy scores will be permanently cleared. Golden Vow used will not be returned. If the other player rejects, you may send another dissolve request within 48 hours of the first, and the relationship will be dissolved directly. A relationship cannot be dissolved within 24 hours of initiating. [FF0000]Deleting a friend will dissolve Dynamic Duo relationship immediately[-].

Cobra Go Guide
Emergency
Shirou gets out of bed to a welcoming warm and sunny day. He stretches thinking, what does today have in store for him? 
Pa-pa-pa-pa-pa. Shirou lets his vector go to deal with the gang members that appeared in front of him. He zoomed out of the tunnel only to be stopped by an armored vehicle. 
All his escape routes were blocked - except for one. Shirou revs his engine and rushes into the enemy circle and tosses a grenade. That will give an opening, but where to next? 
Shirou has found himself in an arena. He tried to deliver the armor to Kla and get out of this situation. But the armor won't come off and now they want him to fight. And the enemy looks formidable. He winds up for the biggest punch of his life. 
It was a day like any other. On a delivery, he met another riding a motorcycle. A young woman with long eyelashes, intense eyes and a leather outfit. Her momentary gaze latched onto him like a fishhook. 
It was another day, driving through the city. Going down a narrow alley and another motorbike was coming up fast, they both swerved and went on their ways. All he saw was a flash of silver hair. 
A delivery had gone wrong, his deliveree was strangling him for no reason. No worries, he's been in worse situations. *Pop* The man yelled and ran back into his house. It was a helmeted woman, she got on her motorcycle and zoomed off. She looked familiar. 
Shirou's mood is high walking about town but is interrupted when a delivery drone drops a package in front of his face. Does Shirou accept the delivery job? 
Shirou opened up the courier package. It was addressed to some guy named Kla, he wondered who he was.
Shirou walks down the street and comes upon a statue of a famous man from the city. Who was he and how did he get his fame? 
All of a sudden wind blew and clouds filled the sky, putting everything in shadow. He turned a corner to escape the weather only to find a motorcycle gang waiting for him. 
Shirou moved quickly, rushed down the stairs as the thugs chased after him. They must want his package. What was in there that was so valuable?
The guys are like a bad flu, he just couldn't shake them. He's found a hiding spot and can't run forever. They'll find him soon. How was he going to get out of this? 
Shirou opens the package, anything useful would help. But what he saw, was not what he expected - armor. He puts it on, and a surge of energy courses through him. 
Shirou runs to find his goal. His beloved motorcycle. The wind streams against his face as he races through the city, the gang on his tail. He's not sure he can outrun them. He'd better create some chaos.

After realizing the dangers of the job with Fast Hurricane, Shirou decided to learn another martial art - Snake Style.

Supplies
Rules
Supply Boxes will consistently appear at spawn points. Transport as much Supply Boxes as possible to ally barracks in order to earn points. Be careful of enemy interception on the way.
Attack and destroy enemy barracks to raid Supply Boxes (each team barracks can only be destroyed once per match). The team whose barracks store the most Supply Boxes wins.

Map code
No map
No map info
No map info found. Please upload map designs from Craftland 2.0
1. Design your own maps and share with other players! \n2. Maps must be uploaded and shared to be used in-game. \n3. You will find your creations in "My Designs". When you click "upload", your map is available to the public and a unique map code will be generated. \n4. Other players will be able to subscribe to your map using the map code or in the room that is playing your map. Once subscribed, any changes you make to the Shared Maps will be automatically updated on their end. \n5. Shared Maps are valid for 180 days, starting from time of upload. Validity countdown will reset if changes are being made to the map. \n6. Any inappropriate content in the map will risk the design and the creator being banned from the game. \n7. Good luck with designing a popular map!

Masked Warlord
Celebrate BOOYAH DAY with your friends!
BOOYAH DAY Guide
LOGIN REWARDS
1. You will be matched with 50 opponents to compete for the BOOYAH DAY Leaderboard.\n2. Roll the dice and move forward on the BOOYAH Go board. You will 10 points with every block you travel. Your accumulated points will determine your leaderboard rank.\n3. Try to earn as much points as possible before [ffc94d]11.27 (19:59:59 UTC)[-] and claim corresponding rank rewards.\n4. The higher your rank, the better your rewards get, so fight your way to the top!\n5. The BOOYAH DAY Leaderboard ranking is updated until [ffc94d]11.27 (19:59:59 UTC)[-]. Rewards can be claimed after ranking stops and before event ends. Any unclaimed leaderboard rewards will be void when the event ends.
1. Obtain BOOYAH Dice in the [ffc94d]Battle Royale[-] mode, [ffc94d]Clash Squad[-] mode,[ffc94d]BOOYAH Go[-], and [ffc94d]daily check in[-]. \n2. Roll the dice in BOOYAH Go to win exciting prizes as well as BOOYAH Heads, which can be used to redeem rewards in the BOOYAH Store. \n3. Defeat the Skull Monster by rolling the dice, earning points, and moving forward. \n4. Battle in Style is a bonus event that will take place during BOOYAH DAY. We will keep you guys posted!

BOOYAH DAY
Scientist spent years developing new tech underground - a suit that would enhance the abilities of its wearer.This special tech was stored in the form of sphere. When activated the enhanced attaches to the activator, enhancing the wearer's strength. The stronger the wearer, the stronger the armor. The levels of strength are represented by different colors.An alpha version was sent to be used in the Booyah day celebration adapted for characters to use in the friendly game.But there were bad actors out there who want to use technology for their own nefarious deeds. Two of these had a plan.Leon was accidentally pulled into the Booyah day celebration, fighting back against the evil-doers.Leon activated the hidden power of the sphere and bore the armor and strength, defeated the evil-doer and became a hero.

Reach {0} in CS Ranked
1. Achieve the designated rank requirement in either BR mode or CS mode to become a mentor and have cadets.\n2. New players and returning players can become cadets and have mentors.\n3. A mentor-cadet relationship can be initiated by either side. The invitee must accept the application for the relationship to start.\n4. At any given time, a mentor may only have 3 cadets while a cadet may only have 1 mentor.\n5. Throughout the mentor-cadet relationship, scores will be awarded and can be accumulated for rewards.
1. After accumulating sufficient scores, a cadet will graduate, and both the mentor and the cadet will receive a graduation present. When a mentor guides sufficient cadets to graduation, he/she will receive an honorable designation.\n2. A mentor-cadet relationship can be ended by either party and will dissolve either when the other party accepts the end request or 24 hours after the end request is raised.\n3. If a mentor does not login to game for a long time, the mentor-cadet relationship will end.
Mentor Rules
You will not be able to have cadets if your rank is too low. Work harder to reach Heroic now!

The island was originally a fishing island that was co-opted as a military outpost during the Great Collapse and was equipped with top-grade military equipment.
The main operations and functions that kept the island functional were all centralized around the port. This was their main connection to the outside world.
This area housed military elites of a previous war. The area was equipped with quality housing and traditional elements from their homeland.
The original resident civilians lived and worked on the island helping contribute to the operations during turbulent time.
Can be used to hold liquids. Produced in the Processing Plant.

During the event period, you can help Professor with his plan of raiding gold from Bermuda through completing missions, collecting Heist Golds in-game and inserting them in the melting pot, and asking friends to help you out. Make sure to keep an eye out on the pot and deliver the melted gold on time. The more you deliver, the more rewards you can get!

Doom/Immortal Zone
When placed, players who enter the trigger zone will experience a specified effect of either immortal or doom. (Not valid in Clash Squad, Team Deathmatch, and Playground modes)
Checkpoint
An item used to build the gameplay in Parkour mode. For the final checkpoint to appear, players must pass by each checkpoint one by one. When eliminated and respawned, player can choose to respawn at the last checkpoint they had passed. (Not valid in Clash Squad, Team Deathmatch, and Playground modes)
Finishing Point
An item used to build the gameplay in Parkour mode. Players must arrive inside the finishing line for the goal to be completed. (Not valid in Clash Squad, Team Deathmatch, and Playground modes)
Spawn Point
No specific team will be assigned to this point. When placed, a random team will spawn here. (Not valid in Clash Squad, Team Deathmatch, and Playground modes)
Large Spawn Point
A large spawn point recommended for Parkour mode, where all players will spawn in a row. (Not valid in Clash Squad, Team Deathmatch, and Playground modes)

Enables size customization

A cuboid or slope whose size can be adjusted

Gem Team
Join now
Travel the world of Free Fire with your friends!
Team up with me
1. Invite friends to your Gem Team. Each team can have a maximum of 4 members. As your team size increases, the team page will also behave differently. \n2. Unlocking Drone Supply to gain exclusive rights for your team. \n3. Once a team is formed, it will be effective throughout the event period. Team members will not be able to leave team, remove other members from the team, or dissolve the team. Please choose your team members carefully. \n4. Players can only form team with other players of the same region.
Rules
Team Progress
1. Team members can participate in Gem Shuffle to obtain Starts. Starts will be accumulated in the team page. You may check on members's contributions in the details page. \n2. When a milestone has been reached, all members can claim rewards. The further the progression, the more Journey Chest Rewards members can claim. \n3. Open Journey Chests to obtain exciting prizes!

Honor System
Dear Survivor, to better monitor our gaming environment, we are introducing the [FEBA00]Honor System[-].\n\nImproper behavior will result in a reduction in the player's honor score, and there is a possibility of getting your access [dc3b3b]limited[-]. Players with good honor scores will be rewarded every week.\n\nPlease maintain a positive gaming attitude and contribute to a fair competitive environment.\n\n[636361]Visit the Honor System page for more details.[-]
OK
We have detected that, due to multiple violations, your honor score is now below {0}. You will now face the following consequence:
You can still play other modes and up your score. Once your score reaches the appropriate level, you can resume playing all modes. Specific rules can be found on the Honor System page.
Since your honor score is below {0}, you are now unable to play in {1}. Please select another mode.
A teammate {0}'s honor score is below {1} and is unable to play {2}. Please select another mode.

Added by Sheriff Robo
Deducted by Sheriff Robo
No improper behavior detected
Total
Players who maintain a perfect Honor Score (100) every week will be rewarded. The situations below will lead to score changes:\n1. Proper gaming behavior will increase the score.\n2. Toxic behavior reports confirmed by the system will decrease the score.\n\n[f15a24]Different scores correspond to different consequences.[-] See details below:\n1. 99-95: No penalties and no rewards.\n2. 94-90: Unable to play CS-Ranked.\n3. 89-80: Unable to play BR-Ranked Duo and Squad mode, and CS-Ranked.\n4. 79-60: Unable to play BR-Ranked and CS-Ranked.\n5. <60 : Unable to play BR-Ranked, CS-Ranked, and cannot team up with other players.
Improper actions, such as AFK, force quit, verbal abuse, and other toxic behavior, will result in different score deductions depending on the game mode. There will be extra deductions for multiple violations that take place on the same day; more violations in one day means more scores deducted.\n\nHonor Score will increase with every match without improper behavior.

Before the Grand Finals, you can select a team that you think will win the competition. If your selection turns out to be correct, you will be rewarded with a grand prize. If not, you will receive a consolation prize.
Before the Grand Finals, Arvon will select one of 12 teams and you will select one of the remaining 11 teams. If your selected team wins the match, you will get a grand prize. Rewards are available even if your selected team does not win. There are 8 matches in total, so make sure to grab your chances!\n\nNote: Results will be updated to Pick 'n' Win 1-2 days after the match is finished. Thank you for your patience.

General Rules
Tutorial
Dear Creator, please be mindful of the following rules when editing your maps:\n1. Commands can only be triggered by incidents. Please ensure that every command is configured with an incident.\n2. Before saving your map, remember to make adjustments so as to avoid any error during matches.\n\nFind more tips in the Craftland Codex. We look forward to seeing your wonderful map designs.

Attack All Around
Thai team, "Attack All Around", crowned world champions at the Free Fire World Series (FFWS) 2022 Sentosa. Overcoming a 15-point deficit in the final game, they defeated last year's champion, "EVOS Phoenix", by one point, taking the final championship from Play-ins as an underdog！

Rampage
This powerful suit was created with the most advanced technology of its time for the director of Rampage, Tyson Brontes.The suit was designed based on all the past Rampage suits, taking the best of each and integrating into one.Tyson's suit accesses the elements of the earth to bolster its main effects - almost impenetrable armor, extreme agility, and powerful strength. Rampage was not always known as Rampage. It started out as Project Harmony, which was created to help rebuild a war-torn city. Tyson was the original director of the project, but when Horizon wanted to move it to a dangerous direction, he put the project to halt and united the soldiers who were originally part of the program. Now, under him, all generations of Rampage answer to his wisdom and protection, so they built him a suit fit for a leader.

Resource path
System resource path is in:path. Custom resource path is out:path. Empty by default.
Color layer
RGBA channel, with each channel taking a value between 0 and 255 saved in int32. Default color is white (255,255,255,255).
Zoom
Zoom scale; default is 1
Position of display
Position of display expressed in global coordinates. Vector3(0) by default.

Community Standards
Thank you for taking the time to read our Community Standards and using the features provided by Craftland. Before creating a map, you must first tap the link below to read the entire Community Standards. After carefully going through and understanding the content, tap the confirm button, which indicates that you accept all the terms stated in the Community Standards.\n\nAfter confirming, you can access and review the Community Standards any time on the settings page in Craftland.\n\nIf you have any questions regarding the agreement, please feel free to contact our customer service.
Read the [FFBA00]Community Standards[-] for more details

1. Create your own maps, design unique challenges and rules, and share the experience with other players!\n2. All designs must be published in order to use them to create rooms for matches.\n3. Share the unique map codes around to invite other players to play the maps you designed.\n4. Published maps are valid for 180 days. If no updates are made for 180 consecutive days, the published map will expire.\n5. Designs and creators with inappropriate content will face bans and punishments.\n6. Flaunt your creativity and build your one-of-a-kind map in Craftland!

Visual effects entity
Continue to next
Skip the current loop iteration and execute the next
Return
Terminates the current execution and returns to the main program. Allows sending back data when returning.
Return value
The data to be sent back to the main program
Compare
Compare 2 values using a designated operator
Left value
The left side value used for the comparison
Comparison function
Select how you want the values compared. Supported operators are: >, ≥, ==, ≠, ≤, <, AND (Boolean only), OR (Boolean only)
Right value
The right side value used for the comparison
Result of the comparison
NOT
Gives the reverse of a logical value
Value
The value you wish to use the NOT operator on
Logical relationship
Result of the NOT operation
Check null
Check whether an entity is null
Entity
The entity to check emptiness for
Whether or not the entity is empty
If
Executes according to whether conditions are being met. The sub-processes are the same as the subsequent logic branches.
Value
The number of conditions and the number of sub-processes (logic branches) must be the same
Loop of integer range
Loop from x to y by a step of z. The sub-process is the loop itself.
Index name
The name of the loop index that can be used to extract the value of a local variable's loop index
Start value
The value at which the loop begins
End value
The value at which the loop ends
Step
The step of each loop. When the start value is less than the end value, step should be greater than 0. When the start value is greater than the end value, step should be less than 0.
Loop every element
Enables traversal of a data container to extract each sub-element in it. The sub-process is the loop itself.
Element
Traversing to this name will extract the value of the local variable's sub-element
Data container
The data container to traverse. Currently supports list types.
Conditional loop
A conditional loop repeats until a condition is met. The loop stops when condition is False.
Condition
Repeats loop if condition is True and stops until condition is False
Break loop
Stops the current loop process and jumps out of the loop

LAB is now available. Check it out!
Legendary outfit now available in LAB!
The Hyperbook system consists of books and pages. In order to proceed with unlocking the pages, you must first obtain the corresponding book.\n1. Books are divided into Hyperbooks, which contain Secret Pages, and regular books.\n2. When you obtain a book, the first page is automatically unlocked. The subsequent pages must be unlocked in chronological order and will have corresponding rewards.[FFBA00]\n3. Hyperbooks contain Secret Pages, which can be unlocked using spins obtained from unlocking the regular pages.[-]\n4. When some regular pages are still locked, unlocking the Secret Page is by luck. If all regular pages are unlocked, unlocking the Secret Page is guaranteed.
Secret Page must be unlocked by spinning
Use spins for chances to unlock the Secret Page. Receive 1 spin whenever a regular page is unlocked. Unlocking all the regular pages will guarantee you the prize on the Secret Page.
Spin for a chance to unlock the Secret Page. Every time a regular page is unlocked, you get a spin chance to unlock the Secret Page.

Tap here to turn on in-game multi-skin
Tap here to read more about this feature
When turned on, you can equip multiple skins. Every match, one of the selected skins will be randomly activated.
In December 2020, Garena Free Fire launched a groundbreaking collaboration that featured the global football superstar, Cristiano Ronaldo, who led the Operation Chrono event as a bounty hunter.
Project Cobra went live in February 2021, revolving around Shirou, a then new character, and a powerful cobra that bestowed the event's protagonist a legendary cobra power.
The Free Fire World Series is Free Fire's biggest global tournament. In May 2021, the second edition of the series is held in Singapore, where the 22 best teams from 14 regions competed in the championship.
The Rampage series returned in 2021 for the third year, running with "Rampage: New Dawn". This time the event took place in a futuristic oriental city filled with despair, and four individuals - Drake, Rajah, Speedy, and Aurora - committed to becoming the vigilantes that the world needed.
In Free Fire 4th Anniversary, a birthday celebration was hosted, featuring world famous DJs, Dimitri Vegas and Like Mike, Alok, and KSHMR, and pushing the definition of a party to a new high.
As a part of the Booyah Day celebrations, Free Fire launched it first ever global brand event that came with multiple in-game modes and fashion items for players to express their personalities and enter battles with creativity so that everyone could "Battle In Style".
In December 2021, survivors were greeted with a blast of cold air as they stepped into a blizzard. In addition to the climatic changes, a new map, an exclusive ranked system for the Lone Wolf mode, and other in-game goodies and activities were introduced in the New Age event.

When entity is created
Triggers when this entity is created
When entity is eliminated
Triggers when this entity is eliminated. The source of attack can be identified using "Trigger Entity" and the type of attack can be identified using "Damage Type".
Triggering entity
The entity who triggers the incident
When button is pressed
Triggers when a button is pressed. "Button Name" is the assigned name and "Trigger Entity" is the player who triggers the button.
Button name
A customized name of a button that triggers an incident
Create item
Create a type of item or a set of items at a specific location. When creating one type of item, the amount is the number of that item. When creating a set of items, the amount is the amount list that corresponds to the item list. The created items will depend on the map's units.
Item list
A list that contains the items that will be created. It can also consist of a single item.
Amount list
An amount list that corresponds to the item list. It may only contain the amount of one item type.

Triggers when a "Trigger Entity" enters a trigger area
The player to get the entity. When linked with the player, the entity's info will only be updated for said player. When no player is linked, all players will receive the entity info.

POTENTIAL
Potential was the underdog of the fashion industry, but despite it all, it overcame the odds and took the world by storm. The brand took its experience to heart and imbued this mentality into its clothing. Not only does the brand allow its wearers to reach their potential, they look amazingly stylish doing so.
Potential allows those who wear its clothing to operate at 100% of their potential to beat the odds.
Unlock your potential
Progress
Reward
TREND+ is a system that compiles outfits and collections that are unique in style. Obtain the outfits and collections of each brand to gain Brand Points, which can be used to redeem exclusive prizes.
SELECTED
Selected wants to be the ultimate expression of individualism. The items it purchases and collects are the most unique and rarest fashion pieces found in the world. Tired of the boring, normalized fashion trends the rest of the world forced on people, Selected wants its wearers to find something that they feel is tailored to them. The brand seeks to separate itself from the rest of the fashion sphere.
Selected wants to be the ultimate expression of individualism.

Modify properties
Modify the properties of entities. [Formulate-able entity] is the entity whose properties you wish to change, including weapons, players, and zombies. [Properties] is used to select the desired property. [Change method] includes adding and subtracting and modifying by percentage. [Value] is the new value. When this script is used multiple times, the change values will accumulate.
The formulate-able entity whose properties to change. Currently supports weapons, players, and zombies.
Weapon entity
The entity property to change. If the selected entity does not have the selected property, then no changes will be applied.
The type of change to be applied, including addition, subtraction, and percentages
Change method
The quantity of the change. To add, enter a positive number. To subtract, enter a negative number. Percentage changes are based on the original property's percentage value.
Currency entity
Currency type

The destination
Return or not
Use to set whether the AI will return to original location after arriving to the destination. TRUE means return; FALSE means do not return.
Attack or not
Use to set whether the AI will attack nearby enemies along the way. TRUE means scan for enemies; FALSE means do not scan for enemies.

Set travel path
AI entities will travel according to the order set here. Available travel types are circle-back, one-way, and round-trip. You can also select whether the AI will scan for enemies on its way.
Path list
A list that consists of the points to which the AI should travel
Travel type
These are the travel types. One-way is for the AI to stop traveling once it arrives at the destination. Circle-back is for the AI to search for a way back once it arrives at the destination. Round-trip is for the AI the return to original location according to the backwards order of the path list.

Angelic is a fashion brand built on the promise of delivering clothing that will get the wearer clicks and views on social media. Angelic prides itself on being the trendsetter that drives the industry. Those that wear their clothes are the brands champions and drive their viralness.
Angelic is the fashion brand of the social savvy consumer who isn't shy about showing their fashion sense to the world.

Densho is handcrafted clothing imbued with the values of never giving up, taking life seriously, but also remembering to live in the moment.

The one-of-a-kind Shield Gun only drops on NeXTerra.
NeXTerra may be small in size but it is big in challenge. Dare to land and fight?
Explore the numerous hidden mechanisms in NeXTerra!
Bounce and fire in NeXTerra's Grav Labs for a next-level shooting experience.
Unlock NeXTerra for a thrilling combat rush by downloading the map from the Download Center in the lobby.
Theres a higher chance of looting level 3 weapons in the central tower at the Intellect Center in NeXTerra!
You can see through the glass panels in NeXTerra's Museum but bullets will not penetrate the glass. Devise clever ways to take your enemies down!
You can use the moving platforms in  NeXTerra to creep up on your enemies. Be careful not to be spotted!
Rumor has it that NeXTerra has a mysterious land where you can jump very high.
NeXTerra has portals that enable you to teleport to different places!
Come to NeXTerra for an intense, thrilling, and fast-paced battle experience!

Set behavior status
Configure the status of different behaviors for players. When disabled, the corresponding buttons on the HUD cannot be used, and manual and automatic movements are not allowed. When hidden, the corresponding buttons will not be displayed on the HUD, and manual movements are not allowed while automatic movements are allowed. When enabled, the corresponding buttons will be displayed on the HUD, and manual and automatic movements are allowed.
The player to set behavior status for
The player's behavior, including moving, attacking, jumping, and sprinting
Status includes disabled, hidden, and enabled. When hidden, the corresponding buttons will not be displayed on the HUD, and manual movements are not allowed while automatic movements are allowed. When enabled, the corresponding buttons will be displayed on the HUD, and manual and automatic movements are allowed.

Open to receive FFWS League Cards
FFWS League Deck Crate
League Deck
Free Fire Arab League (FFAL) is the top league for the Free Fire pro-teams in the Middle East and North Africa .\n\nThe champions of FFAL will represent the Middle East and North Africa region as the first seed, and will compete in the FFWS Finals.\n\nThe 2nd placed team in FFAL will represent the Middle East and North Africa region as the second seed, and will compete in the FFWS Play-ins.
Free Fire Cup is an open tournament for all players in Taiwan. \n\nThe champions of Free Fire Cup will represent the Taiwan region as the 1st seed. They will compete in the FFWS Play-ins.
Free Fire Europe Championship (FFEC) is the highest level of Free Fire Esports competition in Europe.\n\nThe champions of FFEC will represent the Europe region as the 1st seed. They will compete in the FFWS Play-ins.
Free Fire Indonesia Masters (FFIM) is an open tournament for all Free Fire players in Indonesia.\n\nThe champions of FFIM will represent the Indonesia region as the second seed. They will compete in the FFWS Play-ins.
Free Fire League Latin America (FFL) is the ultimate Free Fire tournament in Latin America.\n\nThe champions of FFL will represent the LATAM region as the first seed, and will compete in the FFWS Finals.\n\nThe 2nd placed team in FFL will represent the LATAM region as the second seed, and will compete in the FFWS Play-ins.
Free Fire Master League (FFML)  Divisi 1 is the highest league consisting of 18 professional teams in Indonesia. \n\nThe champions of FFML will represent the Indonesia region as the first seed. They will compete in the FFWS Finals.
Free Fire Pakistan League (FFPL) is the highest tier of tournaments for Free Fire in Pakistan. \n\nThe champions of Free Fire Pakistan League will represent the Pakistan region as the 1st seed. They will compete in the FFWS Play-ins.
The Play-ins will feature 9 teams battling it out to earn a spot at the Finals. The top 4 teams from the Play-ins will join the 8 top-seeded teams, and form the 12 teams who will compete to be winners of the FFWS 2022 Bangkok.\n\nThe teams will battle over 8 rounds – across 4 maps – to be the last one standing, with points awarded based on round ranking and the number of eliminations.
Free Fire Brazilian League (LBFF) is Garena's premier Free Fire competition in Brazil. \n\nThe champions of LBFF will represent the Brazil region as the first seed, and will compete in the FFWS Finals.\n\nThe 2nd placed team in LBFF will represent the Brazil region as the second seed, and will compete in the FFWS Play-ins.
MCPS Majors is the top league for Malaysia, Cambodia, Philippines, and Sri Lanka region. \n\nThe champions of MCPS Majors will represent the MCPS region as the first seed, and will compete in the FFWS Finals.\n\nThe 2nd placed team in MCPS Majors will represent the MCPS region as the second seed, and will compete in the FFWS Play-ins.
Vietnam Free Fire League (VFL) is the highest tier level of Free Fire Esports competition in Viet Nam.\n\nThe champions of VFL will represent the Vietnam region as the first seed, and will compete in the FFWS Finals.\n\nThe 2nd placed team in VFL will represent the Vietnam region as the second seed, and will compete in the FFWS Play-ins.

The Champion of Free Fire World Series 2022 BANGKOK
EVOS Phoenix
FFWS 2022 Bangkok, EVOS Phoenix gained victory in their home country! Becoming the only team who hold the global title twice, under the name Phoenix Force in 2021 and EVOS Phoenix 2022. This is the third time Thailand team continues its global championship legacy. From Play-Ins to Champion with an intense final match, EVOS Phoenix gained 30 points and 18 eliminations, turned the table on Vivo Keyd in the last minute by only 3 points, like their slogan, "Phoenix never dies".

Fan-shaped scope check
Check whether an [entity] is inside a cone. The [center] and [radius] are the center and radius of the cone. The [extracted radius] is the radius of the smaller sector within the cone. The height is the height of the 3D structure. The [angle] is the central angle of the cone. The [direction angle] is the direction vector of the central axis.
The result of the fan-shaped scope check. This is a Boolean value.
Set activity status
Set the activity status of a [target entity]. [Activity status] is a Boolean value and affects the availability, visibility, and collidability of the entity.
Set player's round score
Player visibility
Configure whether the [Setting player] can see the [Target player]. When [Target player] is empty, the setting is applied to all players.
Configure whether the [Setting player] can see the [Target player], such as yes, no, or default. The "Default" setting will rely on the player's own visibility settings. "Yes" or "no" will ignore the player's own visibility settings.

Spherical scope check
Check whether an [entity] is inside a spherical scope. The [center] and [radius] is the center and radius of the spherical structure.
The result of the spherical scope check. This is a Boolean value.
Cubic scope check
Check whether an [entity] is inside the scope of a cube. The [center] is the center of the cube, the [length], [width], and [height] are the length, width, and height of the cube, and the [Euler angles] are the angles of the 3D orientation of the cube.
The result of the cubic scope check. This is a Boolean value.

Switch the camera mode. You can select between No Animation and Progressive Movement.

Obtain current location
Weapon Glory Leaderboard Rules
1. The Weapon Glory Leaderboard displays the [ffba00]Paramount Weapon Glory Titles[-] and the [ffba00]Prominent Weapon Glory Titles[-] based on your location. Your Weapon Glory has an upper limit, which can be raised by reaching higher ranks in ranked modes.
2. The 2 leaderboards are based on the region and the division.
3. Weapon Glory Titles refresh [ffba00]every Monday[-] at 1:00 AM. Titles obtained are retained for a week.
4. Weapon Glory breakdown: The value takes into account the [ffba00]basic score[-], [ffba00]ranked match performance[-], and [ffba00]coefficient[-].\n[ffba00]Basic score[-]: This is converted from the corresponding weapon mastery.\n[ffba00]Ranked match performance[-]: This is calculated based on the overall statistics when using the weapon during ranked matches.\n[ffba00]Coefficient[-]: When a weapon is not used for a long time, its coefficient decreases over time. Increase the coefficient by using the weapon to eliminate enemies during ranked matches.\n[ffba00]Season carry-over[-]: Your Weapon Glory depreciates when entering a new rank season.
5. Geographical Definition Disclaimer:\nThe geographic tiers in the location settings is provided by a third-party map service and solely used for the purposes of the leaderboard. Garena is not responsible for any discrepancies.

Send signal {0}

Create position following motion
Create a position following motion for [Target Entity]. [Target Entity] follows [Following Entity]. [Offset Coordinates] is the coordinate offset between [Target Entity] and [Following Entity]. [Coordinate System] can select World Coordinate System and Relative Coordinate System based on the orientation of Following Entity. [Position Following Motion Entity] will be returned after creating the motion.
Edit visible layers for entity
Edit entity's layer. Set that the camera visible at the corresponding layer to see the entity.
Create following motion
Create a Gaze-following Motion for [Target Entity]. [Target Entity] follows [Following Entity]. [Offset Coordinates] is the coordinate offset between [Target Entity] and [Following Entity]. [Coordinate System] can select World Coordinate System and Relative Coordinate System based on the orientation of Following Entity. [Gaze-following Motion Entity] will be returned after creating the motion.

Rank Unrestricted If Team Full
Rank Limit
1. If the rank gap among players on your team is too large, you will not be able to play ranked matches due to fairness considerations.\n2. Please adjust your team composition based on the upper and lower rank limits. The lower limit indicates the minimum rank of teammates you can match with, while the upper limit indicates the maximum rank of teammates you can match with.\n3. This restriction does not apply when the team is full. If you are unable to start the game, please adjust according to the rules or try inviting more people to fill the team.

Nanotech
With the regenerative power of nanotech, you get another chance to outplay your enemy.
After taking fatal damage, enter an invulnerable and immobile state for [FFB300]{0}s[-]. Then, gain a [FFB300]{1} HP shield[-] that lasts for [FFB300]{2}s[-]. If the skill user knocks down an enemy while the shield is active, restore the same HP amount as the shield's; if not, the skill user will be eliminated. Cooldown: [FFB300]{3}s[-].
After taking fatal damage, consume [FFB300]{4} EP[-] and enter an invulnerable and immobile state for [FFB300]{0}s[-]. Then, gain a [FFB300]{1} HP shield[-] that lasts for [FFB300]{2}s[-]. If the skill user knocks down an enemy while the shield is active, restore the same HP amount as the shield's; if not, the skill user will be eliminated. Cooldown: [FFB300]{3}s[-].
Nano Lifeshield
Deploys a nanotech shield when taking fatal damage and restores the same HP amount as the shield's if the user knocks down an enemy while the shield is active.
Consumes EP and deploys a nanotech shield when taking fatal damage. Restores the same HP amount as the shield's if the user knocks down an enemy while the shield is active.

Rewards given to the winners
[FFB300]Guild Level[-]\n1. Guild level is related to the overall activity of the guild members. Guild level calculations are done every Monday at 4:00 AM. If the activity requirement corresponding to the level is reached at the time of settlement, the guild level will increase.\n2. Every week, the guild needs to consume a certain amount of activity points to maintain the guild level. If the guild's activity is less than the level requirement after the settlement, the guild will be downgraded and the corresponding level rewards will be withdrawn.\n3. The activity points required to maintain different levels are different:\nLevel 1: {0}, Level 2: {1}, Level 3: {2}, Level 4: {3}, Level 5: {4}, Level 6: {5}, Level 7: {6}\n4. Guild size is limited to 20 people at first. This limit can be increased by leveling up the guild or purchasing a guild expansion.\n5. Raising the guild level can also unlock more items in the exchange store, more activity rewards, and more guild icons and icon frames.\n\n[FFB300]Guild Management[-]\n1. Each guild can have 1 Guild Leader, 3 Elders, and 1 Acting Leader.\n2. A Guild Leader can: appoint or demote Elders, approve join requests, kick out members, change the slogan and announcement, change the guild name, expand the guild, and change the guild location. An Elder can: approve join requests, kick out members, change the slogan and announcement, and expand the guild.\n3. All guild members can invite their friends and other players to join the guild. The administrators can send out a maximum of 30 guild invitations per day, and ordinary members can send out a maximum of 10 invitations per day. Players can invite their guildmates using the team-up function.\n4. Members may not leave or be kicked out of the guild within {7} minutes of joining.\n5. When a Guild Leader has been offline for longer than 30 days, to keep the Guild running, members who are in this guild for longer than 7 days may apply to become the Acting Leader. Position is effective upon application. If an Acting Leader is offline for longer than 7 days, their position is automatically invalidated. An Acting Leader has the same authority as the Guild Leader, except for the ability to disband the guild.
[FFB300]Activity[-]\n1. Accumulating Guild Activity Points can level up the guild. Leveling up the guild unlocks more guild privileges, more activity rewards, and more items in the exchange store.\n2. Guild privileges include: additional gold and experience, daily gold limit increased and guild member limit increased.\n3. Ways to earn Activity Points:\na. Daily missions: Each daily mission completion provides {0} Activity Points.\nb. Team up and play matches with guildmates: Teaming up with guild members to participate in any game mode will earn you Activity Points. The longer you play, the more Activity Points you earn.\nc. Guild Wars: Participating in the Guild Wars will earn you a large amount of Activity Points. The longer you participate, the more Activity Points you earn.\n d. Spend diamonds: you will also earn Activity Points when you use diamonds to purchase items (with a weekly limit).\n4. After a player leaves the guild, their My Activity Points will be reset to zero. However, the settled Guild Activity Points will not decrease.
BR Guild Wars\n1. Each Guild Wars season consists of several rounds, with each round opening the Guild Wars mode once. Multiple phases may be included in a round. While you can participate during each phase, the phases all belong to the same round. The round scores earned in the same season will accumulate, and all scores will be reset at the beginning of the next season.\n2. Players must team up with guild members to start matchmaking. Participating in matches will yield participation rewards.\n3. Players can play matches during the match time. At the end of the Guild Wars round, the scores from the player's best {0} matches will be taken, and the sum of the {1} match scores will be the player's total score for this round.\n4. Score calculation: Rank Score + Elimination Score.\na. Rank Score: When a player comes in 1st place in a match, they receive a Rank Score of 12; 2nd place receives a Rank Score of 9; 3rd place receives a Rank Score of 8; 4th place receives a Rank Score of 7; 5th place receives a Rank Score of 6; 6th place receives a Rank Score of 5; 7th place receives a Rank Score of 4; 8th place receives a Rank Score of 3; 9th place receives a Rank Score of 2; 10th place receives a Rank Score of 1; and 11th place and subsequent rankings do not receive any Rank Score.\nb. Elimination Score: Every enemy eliminated in a match is worth 4 points.\n5. After each round, "Guild Honor Titles" will be awarded to the guilds on the leaderboard.\n6. If a player quits their guild or gets banned, the Guild Wars Score they earned will be deducted.\n\nCS Guild Wars\n1. Each Guild Wars season consists of several rounds, with each round opening the Guild Wars mode once. Multiple phases may be included in a round. While you can participate during each phase, the phases all belong to the same round. The round scores earned in the same season will accumulate, and all scores will be reset at the beginning of the next season.\n2. Players must team up with guild members to start matchmaking. Participating in matches will yield participation rewards.\n3. Players can play matches during the match time, with a limit of 7 matches per round. Each victory gives a score of 100; each winning round your team lead gives a score of 1; you will not receive any score if you lose a match.\n4. After each round, "Guild Honor Titles" will be awarded to the guilds on the leaderboard.\n5. Only teams with the same number of victories will be matched together.\n6. If a player quits their guild or gets banned, the Guild Wars Score they earned will be deducted.
[FFB300]Guild News[-]\n1. When guild members meet specific conditions, a message will automatically be triggered and sent to the Guild News channel.\n2. All members can respond to guild news with emoticons.\n\n[FFB300]Star Member[-]\n1. The Guild News channel has a Star Members display board, showcasing outstanding guild members.\n2. Criteria include: Highest BR Winning Streak, Highest CS Winning Streak, Highest BR Rank, and Highest CS Rank.\n3. If multiple players have the same number of winning streak or rank, only the player who meets the conditions first will be displayed.\n\n[FFB300]Guild Announcement[-]\n1. Administrators can edit the announcement on the Guild News page and the Set Guild Info. page. New announcements will be highlighted on the guild overview page.
[FFB300]Member List[-]\nPlayers can view guild member information from the member list. Tap the "Details" button for more information, and you can sort within the guild; administrators can manage members on this page.\n\n[FFB300]Guild Promotion[-]\nGuild administrators can use the guild promotion feature on the Set Guild Info. page. After use, it can increase the priority of the guild on the Guild Recommendation page. Each promotion lasts for 24 hours. The first promotion of each week will consume Gold, and the subsequent promotions will consume Diamonds.

Guild Weekly Activity Rewards
Guild Weekly Activity Rewards can be claimed when the corresponding Guild Weekly Activity progress is reached. Guild Weekly Activity is the sum of all guild members' My Weekly Activity. It resets at 4:00AM every Monday, and the rewards will change according to the guild level.

My Activity Reward
1. My Weekly Activity is the total Activity Points you earned during the week. You can earn Activity Points from the 4 ways shown below.\n2. Accumulate Weekly Activity to claim corresponding Activity Rewards.\n3. Activity Points reset at 4 AM every Monday. Unclaimed rewards will be sent to you via in-game mail, so keep an eye out for them!
Play Matches With Guild Members
1. Team up and play matches with guild members to earn Activity Points.\n2. The more you play together, the more Activity Points you get!

IntelliMate
A new FF Coin Machine has been added to Battle Royale. Interact with it and the entire team will get FF Coins.
Some weapons can be upgraded multiple times with the [FFBA00]Upgrade Chips[-] to become more powerful. Remember to use the Upgrade Chips when you loot them.
Event Info.
A Gloo Wall can aid in fending off enemy attacks. As long as it is successfully deployed, it serves as the best protection during combat.
Is your Gloo Wall deployment always delayed? Go to Settings > Controls > Throwables and turn on the Gloo Wall Smart Throw feature to deploy Gloo Walls faster and evade attacks.
Grenades can cause area damage to enemies from a distance.
If you hear a beeping sound, quickly pay attention to the direction of the red marker. It indicates that an enemy is about to throw a grenade from there!
Alvaro is a demolition artist. Equipping his skill can increase your grenade damage.
Grenade's Beep
Tutorial
Use a Med Kit to restore HP. Remember to remain still and wait until the progress bar is fully completed for the Med Kit to be successfully used.
Inhaler can be used while on the move and simultaneously restores your HP and EP.
A [FFBA00]sub-machine gun[-] has a fast firing rate and short range, making it suitable for attacking enemies at [FFBA00]close range[-].
Don't be afraid when your teammates are eliminated! In Duo/Squad mode, unlock the Revival Card at the vending machine or go to a Revival Point and stay there for a period of time to revive your teammates!
Revive Teammates
A [FFBA00]rifle[-] has high single-shot damage and long range, making it suitable for attacking enemies at [FFBA00]medium to long distances[-].
In a match, the out-of-zone damage will increase over time. Always remember to check the location of the next Safe Zone and get there in time!
A [FFBA00]shotgun[-] has very high single-shot damage, allowing it to quickly inflict a large amount of damage to enemies at [FFBA00]close range[-].
Arsenal
The Arsenal is filled with lots of high-level weapons and items. You can obtain the Arsenal Key by either looting from the ground or purchasing from the vending machines.
Craving for the resources in the Arsenal? Besides looting or purchasing for the Arsenal Key, you can also access the Arsenal by capturing it!
When the In-Match Quest in BR mode is [FFBA00]Defense Arsenal[-], the method of unlocking the Arsenal will change to capturing-based. Upon successful capturing, a team-exclusive UAV will be deployed above the Arsenal.
Got some FF Coins in hand? Go and spend them at the vending machines! You can buy a variety of equipment, and even revive eliminated teammates.
Don't give up when your teammates are eliminated! In Duo/Squad mode, unlock the Solo Dare mission at the vending machine for free and stay alive within the mission time to revive all your teammates!

CHAMPION OF FREE FIRE WORLD SERIES 2023 BANGKOK
Magic Squad
After three weekends of intense competition, Magic Squad was crowned world champion for the first time at the Free Fire World Series (FFWS) 2023 Grand Finals. The team racked up an impressive total of 112 points with 3 Booyahs and 45 eliminations, bringing glory to home ground Brazil. Magic Squad's momentous victory marks the second World Series victory for Brazil, four years after Team Corinthians won FFWS 2019 Rio that took place on their home ground. While rivals from team Thailand put up a strong fight, the team managed to emerge victorious under MGS.BOPS7's leadership and bring home the FFWS championship title.

Defense Airdrop
[FFBA00]Defense Airdrop[-] is a special supply that can be unlocked by players who stay near it for a certain period. The progress bar shows the progress of unlocking the [FFBA00]3 stage rewards[-] of the airdrop.
Discard Item
Your Backpack has a [FFBA00]limited capacity[-]. Once the limit is reached, you cannot pick up more items. Tap the [FFBA00]Backpack button[-] and drag the items you no longer want from the left to the drop area on the right to discard them.
[FFBA00]Landmine[-] can be placed on the ground and will be triggered when enemies approach it. It can also deal damage to the [FFBA00]placer[-]. Be careful not to step on it!

(Unlocks at Lv. {0})
Hunter: Focuses on eliminations and assaults with high flexibility to create chances for the team
Explorer: Focuses on scouting and provides enemy information for the team to gain an edge
Mechanic: Excels in using various items flexibly to create chances on the battlefield
Guardian: Provides healing effects and supplies to help the team survive longer
Balanced: Well-rounded and capable of handling various unexpected situations flexibly

CS Elite
Forms a curved shield that absorbs [FFB300]{0} DMG[-] and lasts for [FFB300]{1}s[-]. The shield will disappear after you fire. When it exists, you can use the skill again to sprint forward for a short distance, during which hit enemies will be knocked over and take [FFB300]{2} DMG[-]. The shield will disappear after the sprint ends. Cooldown: [FFB300]{3}s[-].

Rusher
Rushers love close-range battles. They always approach enemies quickly and eliminate them fearlessly.\n\nGrowth of Expertise:\nUse Shotguns or SMGs to deal enough damage or, even better, eliminate enemies.\n\nFavorite Weapons:\n[ffba00]Shotguns, SMGs[-]
Rusher: Excels at close-range combat with great flexibility and combat endurance
The Role system analyzes your play style based on your battle history. The diagram shows [ffba00]how you play and how often you play in each style[-].\n\nRusher: Uses Shotguns or SMGs to deal close-range damage.\nRifler: Uses Rifles to deal mid-to-long-range damage.\nSniper: Uses Sniper Rifles to deal mid-to-long-range damage.\nBomber: Uses throwables and explosive weapons to deal damage.\nSupport: Heals teammates and scans for enemy locations.
[ffba00]Expertise[-] indicates [ffba00]how well you play[-] in each role. Only when you perform well in a single match can you increase the Expertise of a role.
1. The growth of each Role Expertise is shown here.\n2. The growth level of the Role Expertise depends on your in-match performance.

1. Starting from this patch, Weekly and Monthly Membership have been adjusted and the Super VIP has been removed. Those who have activated Super VIP services can claim compensation items via the in-game mail.\n2. The Membership Discount Store and Daily Rewards (except Diamonds) have been removed.\n3. The new Weekly Membership Lite is now available, which provides rewards at a lower price.\n4. The mechanism of making up for daily check-ins has been optimized. Now you can use Gold to make up for missed check-ins from the past week.

Dear Survivor,\n\nWe understand that you may have encountered some bugs, and we need your assistance to address these issues. To facilitate this, we have given you access to our debug tool, where you can upload your log files to help us identify and resolve the bugs more efficiently. Kindly follow these steps to upload your debug logs:\n1. Reproduce the issue on your device.\n2. Return to the Lobby and go to Settings > Basic > Upload Debug > Upload.\n3. Select the log files as needed (based on the time when the bug was reproduced) > Confirm.\nThank you for helping us make the game better! Should you have any questions, please feel free to contact our customer service.

Score Reward
Participation Reward
A round in Guild Wars may contain multiple phases. Members can choose to participate in any one of the phases or in several phases, while these different phases all belong to the same round. This means that the final score calculation is still based on the round score.

Behavior Rating is calculated based on players' recent in-match performance. Factors leading to a higher rating:\n1. Proper in-match behaviors.\n2. Receiving likes from other players.\nFactors leading to a lower rating:\n1. Negative in-match behaviors, such as quit-outs and toxic behaviors.\n2. Verbal abuse.\nLow rating penalties include:\n1. Extra deduction of Honor Score.\n2. Reduced efficiency in earning CS Rank Protection Points and BR Rank Points.\n3. Muting in voice and chat.\nHigh rating rewards include:\n1. Increased efficiency in earning CS Rank Protection Points and BR Rank Points (up to a  limit).

CHAMPION OF FFWS GLOBAL FINALS 2024 RIO
FLUXO
Representing Fluxo were Bops (Captain), Giuh, BuTziN, MT7, Renan, and coach LUUKING. The victory also marks the second win for Bops, Giuh, and BuTziN, who were part of Magic Squad's winning line-up during the World Series last year. Despite underperforming during the Point Rush Stage two days ago, Fluxo entered the Grand Final looking visibly spurred by the roaring supporters in Rio. The team managed to score back-to-back BOOYAHs in the second and third matches, before eventually seizing the Global Finals throne with one last BOOYAH in the sixth match.

Damage of explosive weapons increases by [FFB300]{0}%[-] and range increases by [FFB300]{1}%[-]. Each grenade will split into [FFB300]{2}[-] mini grenades after explosion, dealing [FFB300]{3}%[-] damage of the original grenade.
Armor durability loss decreases by [FFB300]{0}%[-] and armor DMG reduction increases by [FFB300]{1}%[-]. Obtains a [FFB300]{3}%[-] DMG reduction bonus for each teammate present within [FFB300]{2}m[-].
For every [FFB300]{0}%[-] HP lost, armor penetration increases by [FFB300]{1}%[-] and frontal DMG taken decreases by [FFB300]{2}%[-].
Sprint speed increases by [FFB300]{0}%[-]. After sprinting for [FFB300]{1}s[-], damage of the first [FFB300]{2}[-] shot landed increases to [FFB300]{3}%[-].

Each player can participate in a limited number of matches per week. Each match won gives {0} score, and each winning round your team lead gives {1} score (e.g. A CS match victory with 4:3 has only one lead winning round)
[ffba00]Rules:[-]\nTeam up with guild members and play LW Mode - Guild Wars to earn Guild Honor Titles!\n[ffba00]Time Period:[-]
[ffba00]Rules:[-]\nJoin tournament with guild mates to win Guild Tournament Honor Titles!\nDuring Qualifier Stage, participate in BR Guild Wars to earn required score to qualify for Knockout Stage.\nOnly qualified teams will be matched in Knockout Stage. \n[ffba00]Time Period:[-]
1. Qualifier Stage: \nOpen to all guild members. Sign up and participate in BR Guild Wars to earn score. Reach the required score to qualify for Knockout. \n2. Knockout Stage:\nOnly qualified teams will compete in the Knockout Stage.\nDuring Knockout Stage, the scores of the best {1} matches of the top {0} players in the guild will be added as the total guild score.\n
CS Guild Wars \n1. Each Guild Wars season consists of several rounds, with each round opening the Guild Wars mode once. Multiple phases may be included in a round. While you can participate during each phase, the phases all belong to the same round. The round scores earned in the same season will accumulate, and all scores will be reset at the beginning of the next season.\n2. Players must team up with guild members to start matchmaking. Participating in matches will yield participation rewards.\n3. Players can play matches during the match time, with a limit of {0} matches per round. Each victory gives a score of {1}; each winning round your team lead gives a score of {2}; you will not receive any score if you lost a match.\n4. After each round, "Guild Honor Titles" will be awarded to the guilds on the leaderboard.\n5. Only teams with the same number of victories will be matched together.\n6. If a player quits their guild or gets banned, the Guild Wars Score they earned will be deducted.
Tournament\n1. The tournament consists of Qualifier Stage and Knockout Stage. Each stage may have multiple rounds. During each round, players have to reach the required score to advance and go for a higher ranking. The score will reset at the beginning of the next round.\n2. Players must team up with guild members to start matchmaking. \n3. Players can play matches during the match time. At the end of the Guild Wars round, the scores from the top {1} players' best {0} matches will be taken, and the sum of the {2} match scores will be the player's total score for this round.\n4. Score calculation: Rank Score + Elimination Score.\na. Rank Score: When a player comes in 1st place in a match, they receive a Rank Score of 12; 2nd place receives a Rank Score of 9; 3rd place receives a Rank Score of 8; 4th place receives a Rank Score of 7; 5th place receives a Rank Score of 6; 6th place receives a Rank Score of 5; 7th place receives a Rank Score of 4; 8th place receives a Rank Score of 3; 9th place receives a Rank Score of 2; 10th place receives a Rank Score of 1; and 11th place and subsequent rankings do not receive any Rank Score.\nb. Elimination Score: Every enemy eliminated in a match is worth {3} points.\n5. After the season ends, "Guild Honor Titles" will be awarded to the guilds on the leaderboard.\n6. If a player quits their guild or gets banned, the Guild Wars Score they earned will be deducted.

[b]Gold Royale[/b]\n1. Spin for the Grand Prize with Gold or Gold Royale Vouchers.\n2. You will get 1 random item for each spin, regardless of whether you use Gold or Gold Royale Vouchers.\n3. You are guaranteed to win the Grand Prize/one of the Grand Prizes when you reach the maximum Lucky point.\n\n[b]Diamond Royale[/b]\n1. Spin for the Grand Prize with Diamond or Luck Royale Vouchers.\n2. You will get 1 random item for each spin, regardless of whether you use Diamond or Luck Royale Vouchers.\n3. You are guaranteed to win the Grand Prize/one of the Grand Prizes when you reach the maximum Lucky point.\n\n[b]Item Tier[/b]\n1. Spin for the Grand Prize with Diamonds.\n2. You will win at least one Grand Prize in 50 spins or less.\n3. There are no repeated Grand Prizes. Universal Ring\n1. Spin and win Universal Ring Tokens or prizes.\n2. Universal Ring Tokens can be used to exchange for items in the Exchange Store.\n3. Universal Ring Tokens can be saved for future Universal Ring events.\n4. If you win a permanent item that you already own, it will be converted to Universal Ring Tokens. The number of the converted tokens will be half of the item's cost in Exchange Store (e.g. If the item costs 20 tokens, you will receive 10 tokens as fallback).\n\nEVO Vault\n1. Spin for a chance to win a Lv. 1 EVO Gun.\n2. You are guaranteed to win one EVO Gun in every 50 spins.\n3. If you win an EVO Gun that you already own, it will be converted to EVO tokens of the corresponding EVO Gun and EVO gun exchange token.\n4. EVO gun exchange token can be used in the EVO Vault exchange store to exchange for EVO guns or EVO weapon universal tokens.\n5. The total value of the EVO tokens fallback is equivalent to 50 x Single Spin Cost.\n\nPremium Ring\n1. Spin and win event-specific tokens or Grand Prizes.\n2. Grand Prizes are not available in the Exchange Store and can be won from spins only. You are guaranteed to win one Grand Prize in every 200 spins.\n3. Event tokens can be used to exchange for items in the Exchange Store. You can also exchange for Premium Ring Vouchers which can be used to spin in this or subsequent Premium Ring events.\n4. If you win a permanent item that you already own, it will be converted to event-specific tokens and can be exchanged for other prizes in the Exchange Store.\n\nBooyah Pass Ring\n1. Spin and win the Booyah Pass Premium Plus or BP Ring Tokens.\n2. BP Ring Tokens can be used to exchange for the Booyah Pass Premium or Premium Plus in the Exchange Store.\n3. BP Ring Tokens can be saved for future BP Ring events (1st - 7th of each month).
Evo Vault\n1. Spin to win: Evo Gun (Lv. 1), Universal Evo Tokens, FF Vault Tokens, or other prizes from the ring on the left.\n2. Within every 100 spins, an Evo Gun (Level 1) is guaranteed to drop.\n3. FF Vault Tokens can be exchanged for Evo Gun (Lv. 1) of choice, or Universal Evo Tokens, from the Exchange Store on the right.\n4. Winning an Evo Gun you already own converts the Evo Gun into FF Vault Tokens.\n5. FF Vault Tokens can be used universally in all Evo Vault.\n\nMulti-Ring Token (MRT) Ring (indicated by golden hexagon icon)\n1. Spin to win: Prizes (directly), Multi-Ring Tokens (MRT), or other prizes from the ring on the left.\n2. Multi-Ring Tokens (MRT) can be exchanged for Prizes from the Exchange Store on the right.\n3. Multi-Ring Tokens (MRT) can be used universally in all Multi-Ring Token (MRT) Rings.\n4. For most Prizes, winning ones you already own will convert them to Multi-Ring Tokens (MRT).\n\nSpecial Ring\n1. For most Prizes, winning ones you already own will convert them to Ring Tokens.

[b]Event Rule[/b]\n1. Use diamonds to spin once or a round at the Basic Prize Pool. If you spin a round and win the Grand Prize before all the spins are used, the unspent diamonds will be refunded.\n2. You will not win the same Basic Prize twice. After the Grand Prize is won, the remaining Basic Prizes will be awarded, and the Basic Prize Pool will reset.\n3. Win the Grand Prize to get a random Premium Prize. Prizes obtained will not be repeated.\n\n[b]Item Tier[/b]\n1. You can identify the tier of an item based on the background color under its item name.\n2. "[ED1717][b]Artifact[/b][-]" tier items have "[ED1717][b]red backgrounds[/b][-]".\n3. "[FFA302][b]Mythic+[/b][-]" tier items have "[FFA302][b]striped orange backgrounds[/b][-]".\n4. "[FFA302][b]Mythic[/b][-]" tier items have "[FFA302][b]orange backgrounds[/b][-]".\n5. "[D048CC][b]Epic+[/b][-]" tier items have "[D048CC][b]striped purple backgrounds[/b][-]".\n6. "[D048CC][b]Epic[/b][-]" tier items have "[D048CC][b]purple backgrounds[/b][-]".\n7. "[4DA2F9][b]Rare[/b][-]" tier items have "[4DA2F9][b]blue backgrounds[/b][-]".\n8. "[46DA51][b]Uncommon[/b][-]" tier items have "[46DA51][b]green backgrounds[/b][-]".\n9. "[EDEDED][b]Common[/b][-]" tier items have "[EDEDED][b]grey color backgrounds[/b][-]".

1. AI generate now supports more scene themes. You can also compare scenes before and after generation.\n2. Kalahari and empty scene map are now available for editing and use.\n3. Added waypoint feature for more efficient object path editing.\n4. Plot can now be set up for dialogues, with options added directly to dialogues that can trigger different logic branches based on the choices. Currently, plot can only be triggered with blocks. Similarly, dialogue events only be used in blocks.\n5. Grouped objects can now be saved as templates.\n6. AI assistant now supports setting game parameters and creating in-game stores.\n7. Added 6 new geometric shapes for design.

Prime Level Details
Prime Level
Your Points: {0}
{0} Points
1. Prime is an exclusive status that you can unlock for the first time by obtaining just {0} diamonds.\n2. There are 8 Prime Levels, each offering unique privileges. Higher levels include all the privileges of the lower levels.\n3. Your Prime level increases as you earn Prime Points, which are accumulated over your account's history.\n4. Prime Score is increased by obtaining diamonds (1 diamond = 1 point).\n5. Once you become a Prime player, you need to increase your Prime Score by any amount each month to maintain your current Prime level.\n6. If your Prime Score is unchanged in the entire month, you will drop down 1 Prime level on the first day of the next month at 4 am. However, you will not drop lower than Level 1.\n7. If your Prime Level drops, you can instantly restore it to the highest level you've previously reached by increasing your Prime Score by any amount.\n8. The Monthly Prime Leaderboard will reset on the first day of each month at 4am, the Yearly Prime Leaderboard on first day of the year at 4 am.\n9. The anonymous feature on Prime Leaderboard will take effect 10 minutes after turning it on.

Overview
Solara added to available maps
1. Added a vast collection of assets to the Asset Store. \n2. Users can now quickly save scene objects as templates. \n3. Templates can now be published to the Asset Store or shared with others using a generated share link.
1. Added a new camera object and related settings.\n2. Added restore button in the scene.\n3. Fixed the issue where the camera height slider was difficult to drag when at the very top or bottom. \n4. Optimized the status when selecting an NPC.
1. New themes added: Outdoor Cinema, Tactical Field.\n2. Optimized the edges for generated scenes when using special shapes and freehand drawing.
1. Switch bar added for users to switch between block scripts in the editing page. \n2. Users can create and edit client block script, which is mainly suitable for local effects (such as special effects, interactions) and other features. \n3. Added a new create list block and more variable types. \n4. Added search feature in the drop-down menu for blocks.
UI Widgets can now be copied. \n\nMore update contents are waiting for you to discover. \nStay tuned for more powerful features in the future!

Advanced Settings
Block Script does not support Client Block Script API
Client Script
Client Block Script does not support Block Script API
The Client Scripts handle local effects (such as special effects and interactions) and do not impact the core rules. Unlike Block Scripts, they are solely designed to enhance the user experience and reduce the server's workload. Choose the script type that best suits your needs. [FFFFFF]Due to restrictions of client execution, some blocks cannot be used in the client-side block scripts. [-]

Special Task
Area locked. Unlock to claim rewards.
1. At the start of the event, you will randomly land in a designated area of the new map, Solara, to begin your exploration.\n2. Earn tokens by completing daily tasks or playing matches.\n3. Use tokens to explore the area you are currently in.\n4. Once your exploration progress reaches 100%, you can move on to the next area.\n5. For every area you fully explore, you will receive a Progress Reward.\n6. Each area has a special task, and completing it will grant you additional rewards.\n7. The detail page for each area will be periodically updated with the number of eliminations recorded in that area during the match. Don't forget to like and follow for updates!

CS-Peak is ending soon
A new CS-Peak season has started!
Get [x0.4][S0][-][FFBA00]{0}[-] more to unlock [FFBA00]CS-Peak[-]
Peak mode not open at the moment
[FFBA00]1. Qualifications[-]\n1.1 Rank: The participant must have reached a certain rank in the season in order to play the Peak mode.\n1.2 Time: The participant can only play Peak matches from 6:00 pm to 12:00 am.\n\n[FFBA00]2. Peak Points[-]\n2.1 After a Peak match ends, players' Peak Points will change based on their in-match performance and the match results.\n2.2 The initial Peak Points start at 1200. The Peak Points from the last season will be partially passed down to the next based on certain rules.\n2.3 Peak Rewards will be available when players obtain the corresponding Peak Points and will be reset once the new season starts.\n\n[FFBA00]3. Matchmaking Rules[-]\n3.1 The Peak mode is by default solo play. All players stay anonymous starting from the loading page until the current Peak match ends, after which their names are revealed.\n3.2 No simulators are allowed in the Peak mode.

Get Gyroscope Orientation
Device orientation in 3D space, representing rotation angles around the three axes.
Get Gyroscope Gravity
The gravity component detected by the sensor on your device
Get Gyroscope Rotation Rate
This refers to the rotation rate of a device. It is a term related to the gyroscope sensors that describes the device's rotational movement speed in a three-dimensional space.
Get Gyroscope Rotation Unbiased
Describes the device's rotation rate and provides a more precise result by compensating for the noise or bias of the device's sensors.
Get Gyroscope User Acceleration
The part of the device's acceleration with the gravity component removed, which is the acceleration caused by user actions or external forces.

1. Players can earn Peak Energy by performing well during matches. The energy can be used to offset an equivalent amount of Peak Points lost when the player faces a defeat.\n2. If a player's Peak Points are below [FFBA00]{0}[-], their Peak Energy after a victory can be converted to bonus points (up to the same amount of the original points).\n3. Once a player's Peak Points exceed [FFBA00]{1}[-], their Peak Energy can no longer be converted or increased.\n4. Peak Energy earned during a match cannot be immediately used for that match.

User Message To Persona Voice Response
Send persona user's message and get a voice response from persona

Send persona system message and get a voice response from persona

Create Entity
Create an entity. You can specify an owner (a specific player client). In combination with the AddComponent API, you can synchronize data targeted to that particular client.
The target entity to be created
The type of the created custom component
The owner of the entity
Flag
Entity flag. See Entity Flag enum for more details. Can be used in combination.
Set Material Property
This API allows you to modify certain properties of an object's material.
The target object to get the material property for
Property Name
Set the name of the property
Property Value
Set the value of the property
Success
Set whether it's successful or not
Clear Material Property
Clear the set properties
The target object to clear the material property for
On Custom Property Change
When a monitored custom property changes, this event will trigger. Note: if a custom property is modified multiple times within a single frame, this event is only triggered once. When the server modifies a custom property, the event is sent to both endpoints; when a client modifies a local entity's custom property, the event is only delivered locally.
Property Index
The property's index
The property's new value
On Custom Player State Changed
This event will trigger when the state of the custom player changes
Custom Player State
The state of the custom player
On Receive Voice Response From Persona
Trigger this event when persona's voice response is received
Sender
On Play Voice Finish From Persona
Trigger this event when persona's voice response has finished playing
Bound Custom Player
When bound to a Custom Player, the classic character model is hidden, and the target object's position, rotation (facing), and other data are synchronized to it.
