Pause Time
Knockback Power
The impact player will receive when colliding with an obstacle
Rotate Axis
The axis of this object's rotation
Runtime
This object will move for a period of time and then rest for a period time in cycles (in milliseconds)
Sleep Time
This object will be active after a set time (in milliseconds) after the game starts
Speed
Stun Time (ms)
The time that the player is unable to control the character after hitting an obstacle
Interpolate
Blend Ratio
Adjust the proportion of predicted values applied. For example, 0.8 means using 20% of the current data and 80% of the predicted data as the final result.
Extrapolation
Interpolation
Interpolation Method
Extrapolation: The object's position is slightly ahead of its actual position, which may cause brief penetration.\nInterpolation: The object's position is slightly delayed compared to its actual position, which may reduce some collision jitter.
Max. Error
If the error exceeds this value, it will be corrected immediately (occasionally resulting in an effect similar to teleportation).
Error Correction Time
When the error exceeds this value, a timer will start based on this value, and the error will be forcibly corrected (resulting in an effect similar to teleportation).
Error Threshold
When the error exceeds this value, a timer starts, and the error is then forcibly corrected (resulting in an effect similar to teleportation).
Predict Ratio
Adjust the extrapolation predict ratio. A value of 1.2, for example, will increase the result by 20%.\nIf an object's speed changes frequently, this value should be appropriately reduced.

1. Craftland Creator Center is now online! In the Creator Center, Creators can: manage the current and associated accounts, centrally manage and publish maps across regions to other servers, view detailed data of published maps, check storage usage for maps with data storage feature.\n\n2. The Performance Editor debuts! The Performance Editor is a powerful tool for story performances, supporting precise timeline-based control over elements such as object, sound effects, and cameras in the scene. With it, you can easily achieve animation effects, sound triggers, camera switches, and more to create a more immersive game experience.
1. Optimized block editor's performance and structure for smoother, more efficient use.\n\n2. Added template feature for users to add, use and manage templates more quickly.\n\n3. Some expandable blocks can now be freely expanded or collapsed.
1. Optimized scene grid feature with the new option to enable or disable grid snapping.\n2. Added a movement restriction option for spawn point objects, allowing players to freely explore the map after leaving the spawn point.\n3. Adjusted the structure of the property panel by introducing the concept of component, making the categories more intuitive.\n4. Optimized custom properties, and expanded the scope of custom components.\n5. Custom objects can now attach physical components, as rigid bodies.\n6. Upgraded and optimized the Skybox feature, integrating it into the environment sky feature.
1. Added debugging feature: Skip current phase. This allows direct skipping of game preparation phase, round preparation phase and more during debugging.\n2. Debug window optimized with more filter options for higher debugging efficiency.\n3. Optimized asset details page in the asset store, with a new report feature added.\n4. Craftland now supports mid-game player joining, and users can set the minimum starting players for more flexibility on game start and matching.\n5. Global physics parameters can now be set for unified control of in-game physics performance.\n\n\nMore powerful features are in development, stay tuned!

Expire in {0} days
A map will remain active for 180 days after it's published. After that, it will expire and become unavailable to players. Saving and updating the map during the active period will reset the active days. If a map has already expired, editing, saving, and updating it will reactivate it.

Scene View
The performance editor has two views: Scene View and Track View. In Scene View:\n1. Directly select a scene object to create a track for it, or tap the Create Track button to do so.\n2. When a keyframe is selected or when the Record button is enabled, use scene editing tools to edit.\n3. In this view, you can precise edit a track and preview the effects quickly.
Track View
The performance editor has two views: Scene View and Track View. In Track View:\n1. Tap the Create track button to create a track.\n2. When a keyframe is selected or the record button is enabled, open the content panel to edit.\n3. In the view, you can see more tracks at once, making it easier to align content on the tracks.
Create Track
In the performance editor, you can manage actors, play sound effects, and more through individual tracks. Each track functions like a crew member in a production team. Currently, the performance editor provides the following tracks:\n1. Actor Track: Almost all scene objects can serve as actors. You can use this track to control an object's movement from one point to another, as well as its rotation and scaling. Additionally, you can use this camera for cinematic movements and preview effects in real time.\n2. Audio Track: Select this track and add audio clips to play specified sound effects at specific times.\n3. NPC Track: In addition to controlling movement, rotation and scaling like objects, you can also create animation subtracks that allow NPCs to play various animations continuously.\n4. Event Track: If you want a specific block script to run at a specific event, this track helps you send custom events at keyframe times.\n5. Switch Camera Track: Just like different camera angles in movies, this track help you achieve that if you choose different cameras when creating the clip.\nIn addition to the above tracks, we also support subtracks such as Along Path Track, Animation Track, and more!
Toolbox
When you select a track, you will see a toolbox with tools you can use, such as deleting the track, adding a subtrack, adding a keyframe, or adding a key clip.\nWhen you select a keyframe or key clip, you can use tools like deleting the keyframe or opening up the content panel.\nNote that the position, rotation and scaling tools can only be used when the recording is enabled or when a keyframe is selected.
Console
In the console, you can (in order from left to right on the buttons): \n1. Switch the selected track\n2. Player view: When player view is enabled, you can fully preview the screen that the players see during gameplay.\n3. Record button: IF enabled, you can edit the actor's position, rotation, and scale at any time, and these properties will be automatically recorded as keyframes.\n4. Play and jump: Similar to a video player, you can pause at any time, move forward 1 sec, move back 1 sec\nIn addition to the above functions, you can also:\n1. Drag the track to change the time.\n2. Press and hold the keyframe or key clips to move them.\n3. More features are waiting for you to explore!
Play performance
You can play the performance you created by one of the two ways:\n1. Find Performance Trigger in the object bar and edit the object property so that any player entering the trigger will play the corresponding performance.\n2. Use a block script to create a performance.

Manage Components
Manage Components\n1. On the left is the component list. Select a component to create property and create/edit script.\n2. The central area is for creating component's property. Tap "+" to create a custom property for the corresponding component. All entities using this component will have the new property added.\n3. At the bottom right section, you can find a button for script. You can create or jump to the corresponding script for the selected component. This script will apply to all entities using this component.\nNote: Components and entities are two different concepts. The properties and scripts here will apply simultaneously to all entities that are using this component, not individual objects or items.

NERO & YETI'S STORY
Free Rewards Await
1. Complete daily tasks or play matches to get event tokens.\n2. Use tokens to weave Yeti's dreams.\n3. Each time you enter Yeti's dream, you can obtain different dream elements.\n4. Choose different elements to generate various dreams, and collect and apply your preferred one.\n5. The dream you apply will change your event page and photo background.\n6. The dreams you collect will be stored in your dream archive, where you can browse or delete them.\n7. When weaving dreams, you may trigger a candy rain. Grab the candies to earn rare rewards like stickers and frames.\n8. You can save your favorite dreams to your album.\n9. When you weave enough dreams, you'll not only earn milestone rewards but also receive an exclusive title.

Connect the blocks!
Fill in the parameters to perform all kinds of game logic!
Must be attached to a custom trigger object. Make sure the trigger is placed on the ground to prevent unexpected behavior. First, adjust the player limit parameter above.\nA sound effect plays each time a player enters.\nWhen the last player enters the trigger, a horizontal barrier is automatically generated to block entry and exit, and a red visual effect is played.\nTo extend the logic, follow the instructions and add your logic below the template.
Must be attached to a player. First, set the item to be limited and the quantity.\nEach template can limit only one item type by default, but the template can be used more than once.\nIf a negative number is entered for the limit, it will be automatically set to 0.\nA limit value greater than the template's default limit has no effect.\nWhen the limit is reached, the player's ability to pick up items will be disabled for 2 seconds.
Can be used in any script.\nPass in the player and an icon that follows that player will be created above their head.\nTo change the icon, size, color, or other parameters, modify them after applying the template.
Can only be used in block scripts.\nYou can adjust the countdown duration, the displayed prompt text, and the parameters to pass in.\nUse placeholders such as {0}, {1} in the description field and provide a list of parameters in the same order to insert their values into the prompt text. \nEven if you only need one parameter, it must be supplied as a list.

In-Match Gameplay
Keep cheering to trigger a combo!
1. Complete daily tasks and in-match event tasks to earn event tokens. Use tokens to cheer. Cheering repeatedly can trigger a combo effect.\n2. Cheering raises the show's popularity and unlocks milestone rewards.\n3. When the show reaches Milestones 2 and 4, new songs and stage backgrounds are unlocked. You can switch among unlocked songs at any time.\n4. Unlocked stage backgrounds also become event-only photo backgrounds in Camera System. Use them to take photos with ALOK.\n5. Open the in-match gameplay page to view special event gameplay instructions and complete special in-match event tasks to obtain special event tokens.

{0} Squad Glory
Squad Glory Season
[FFBA00]1. Qualifications[-]\n1.1 Rank: The participant must have reached a certain rank in the season in order to play the Peak mode.\n1.2 Time: The participant can only play Peak matches from 6:00 pm to 12:00 am.\n\n[FFBA00]2. Peak Points[-]\n2.1 After a Peak match ends, players' Peak Points will change based on their in-match performance and the match results.\n2.2 The Peak Points from the last season will be partially passed down to the next based on certain rules.\n2.3 Peak Rewards will be available when players obtain the corresponding Peak Points and will be reset once the new season starts.\n\n[FFBA00]3. Matchmaking Rules[-]\n3.1 The Peak mode is squad play only, and the matchmaking is based on the highest Peak Points within the 4-player team.\n3.2 All players stay anonymous starting from the loading page until the current Peak match ends, after which their names are revealed.\n3.3 No simulators are allowed in the Peak mode.

Online Log
Records the online log. This works the same way as LogInfo, but calls are not stripped by the compiler and will be recorded on online servers. For server security, OnlineLog enforces a per‑frame write limit. When that limit is exceeded, the write will fail and the dropped entry is recorded as an intercepted log.
The content to log. Accepts any object type.

Switch Weapon Fixed Time
Fixed total time required to switch to this weapon (in seconds). If set to 0, the switch duration = PreSwitchWeaponTime + PostSwitchWeaponTime.
Pre-Switch Weapon Time (s)
Time for the preparation phase before switching weapons (in seconds), typically used to holster the current weapon. If SwitchWeaponTime = 0, the total switch duration = PreSwitchWeaponTime + PostSwitchWeaponTime.
Post-Switch Weapon Time (s)
Time for the completion phase after switching weapons (in seconds), typically used to draw the new weapon and enter the ready state. If SwitchWeaponTime = 0, the total switch duration = PreSwitchWeaponTime + PostSwitchWeaponTime.
Scope In/Out Transition Time (s)
Scope in/out transition time (s), must be greater than 0.
Control UI or widget's visibility. When this property is set to false, the widgert's collision area is also disabled, which may cause malfunction.

Newbie Preset

The Craftland homepage now features a map recommendation section, showcasing all maps created by creators. Choose your favorite map and play with friends!
1. New gameplay mode: Party Parkour. In this mode, players control mini characters and experience a brand-new parkour adventure. This mode is still in testing (some APIs do not work in this mode. Please refer to the tutorial for details).\n2. The classic Bermuda map is now available in Craftland. Iconic areas such as Old Peak and Rim Nam Village have returned, waiting for you to explore!\n\n3. Added a New Year theme to AI generation, and a new racing theme: downhill track to parkour templates.
1. Added a new map gameplay type option. When publishing, you can select a gameplay type for the map to help the system recommend the map more accurately to players who may enjoy that gameplay.\n2. When publishing or updating a map, a new information completeness prompt pop-up has been added. Only maps with complete information will receive exposure and push notifications on the homepage.\n3. The map code now features a short code function. Newly published or updated maps will automatically be assigned a short code, making sharing easier. The original long map code can still be used.
1. Selected objects in the scene will remain synchronized with the Object Manager.\n2. Object Manager optimization: now supports hiding, locating, and multi-selecting objects within the Object Manager.\n3. Multi-selection of objects now supports box selection, making batch editing more efficient.
1. Asset Store now supports textures, sound effects, and more. Plenty of texture and music resources will be available. Creators can use them, along with basic geometrical shapes, sound effect entities, block logic, and more, to enrich their maps!\n 2. In future versions, a custom upload feature will be available, allowing creators to upload their own resources and have more freedom in map creation.
1. Added a permanent photo button, allowing players to take and share photos in Craftland. Shared images include map information and can be saved locally or viewed in the in-game album.\n2. Added new block templates.\n3. Text widgets in the UI editor now support bold, italic, and underline font styles.\n4. Zombies' limbs can now be torn off by dealing DMG to them for a more realistic effect.\n5. Optimized component display logic: components that cannot take effect temporarily are now hidden, making the editor interface cleaner and more user-friendly.\n\nDiscover more new content and stay tuned for more powerful features in the future!

Due to your amazing works in Craftland's Asset Store, we've increased your upload and download limits!
Craftland Asset Store Privilege
1. All your local assets will be displayed here, including those downloaded from the store, generated by the editor, uploaded by yourself, and more.\n2. Assets such as images and sound effects can be directly used in basic geometrical blocks, sound effect entities, or block scripts.\n3. Store assets will be downloaded locally, and even if you cancel a purchase, the asset will not disappear.\n4. You can use filters to view which assets are used in the current map.\n5. Deleted assets can always be re-downloaded from your purchase history or recreated and uploaded again.

Knockdown Streak
Releases the sunpower forward in a [FFB300]{0}m[-] fan-shaped range to tag the first undowned enemy hit for [FFB300]{1}s[-] and creates a link with them (visible in [FFB300]{2}m[-] to only the skill user and their tagged enemy). If the user reduces the tagged enemy's HP to [FFB300]{3}[-] or below, the enemy is knocked down instantly. If the enemy is knocked down or eliminated while tagged, moonpower will reset the skill and restore the user's HP by [FFB300]{5}/s[-] for [FFB300]{6}s[-] in total. Cooldown: [FFB300]{4}s[-].

Links to a target ahead and knocks them down at low HP. If successful, the skill resets and the user's HP is restored.

Its body refracts deep-sea light like dream crystals, and it moves like a shifting crystal mountain.
Prismatic Whale

Phantom Manta
It wears layered, crystal-like plates that sparkle among the coral.
Glassback Seahorse
Its scales are glassy and translucent with flowing streaks of light; it is lively and agile.
Iridescent Clownfish
Thin as glass, it scatters tiny gleams as it swims.
Crystal Butterflyfish

Rockplate Turtle

Amber Clownfish

1. Complete daily tasks to earn event tokens.\n2. Spend tokens to summon sea creatures, unlock entries in the Sea Creatures Guide, and increase your event progress. Reach milestones to unlock rewards.\n3. After all milestone rewards have been claimed, tokens can continue to be used to obtain additional repeatable rewards.\n4. Unlock the required number of entries in the Sea Creatures Guide to claim special rewards.\n5. Unlocking rare sea creatures grants bonus tokens. This bonus can be claimed once per day and will not carry over, so be sure to claim it on time.\n6. To ensure smooth performance, the number of sea creatures displayed during the event is limited. Sea creatures that are not shown still exist, and the actual totals and extra token rewards are unaffected.

Autosave size: {0}, limit: {1}
The current property does not support character controller
Data Validity Check
Once enabled, players will undergo strict data validity checks, such as not being allowed in two matches simultaneously.
After autosave is enabled, players' attributes will be saved and carried over to the next match. Note: the max length of a single attribute cannot exceed 4kb. You can also go to the Creator Center to view or modify data (modifying data requires the player to be offline. To avoid display issues, please use English when naming attributes). Check out the official website for more details.

If no new status packet is received in 1 second, is Out Of Sync = true; receiving a packet sets is Out Of Sync = false.

Enumeration of reasons for the ending of an item's use. Used as parameters for the  On Use End event.

Consumed

Equip loadouts for strong combat capabilities!
A124 can unleash an electromagnetic wave. All enemies hit by it will not be able to use their active skills.
Alok's active skill heals you over time and makes you run faster. Teammates can pick up the music notes you've dropped and get the same effects.
Try to activate the skill beforehand for the team speed boost. Not too early though, the effects might wear off when battle begins.
Alvaro boosts the damage of your explosive weapons and grenades. After thrown, each grenade will split into 3 mini grenades.
Andrew makes your armor sturdier and reduces damage taken. The more teammates around you, the higher the damage reduction.
Antonio offers extra protection with the additional Shield Points
Caroline allows you to run faster while holding a shotgun
Chrono's active creates a dome of protection that blocks incoming bullets
You can use Clu's active to scan for enemies and display their locations on the minimap. This info is also shared with your teammates.
When you can't spot any nearby enemies, use Clu's scan to reveal their positions on the minimap.
Dasha makes you run and shoot faster after a knockdown. The effect will stack for knockdown streaks!
D-bee makes you run faster and shoot more accurately when you're firing on the move
Dimitri's skill creates a special zone that heals or auto helps up teammates inside
Use it near a downed teammate to help them up, or on a 1v1 for a second chance after you're knocked down.
When used with Thiva's skill, teammates downed inside the healing zone will get up faster.
Ford restores a small amount of HP slowly after you're hit
Hayato allows you to deal higher damage to armored enemies when you're low on health. You'll also take less frontal damage.
Homer releases a scout drone that flies towards the nearest enemy and explodes
If you want to check if an area is safe or not, release Homer's drone to scout any hidden enemies.
When paired with Moco's passive, enemies hit by the drone will be briefly marked.
Use Ignis' skill to unleash a flaming screen forward and deal burning damage to Gloo Walls or enemies in contact
Reposition or reset your team while the flaming screen is blocking enemies' vision
After deploying the flaming screen, tap the skill again to make it stay at its current position.
When Iris' skill is activated, your shots can pierce through Gloo Walls with reduced damage and mark enemies behind.
Jai will auto-reload your weapon after you use an active or knock down an enemy
J.Bieb's skill allows you and your nearby teammates to block some damage with EP. Teammates' lost EP will be returned to you.
Joseph makes you immune to any debuffs from enemies, such as slow effects and enemies' mark.
Bring Jota with you to recover a small amount of HP when you hit an enemy, and recover more after a knockdown.
K's skill allows you to switch between two modes: Jin-jitsu Mode for slow EP recovery, and Psychology Mode for faster EP conversion.
Kairos will restore your EP over time. When EP is full, your next attack deals extra damage to enemies' armor and shield.
Kapella allows you to heal teammates with your bullets
Kassie lets you form a healing bond with a teammate to heal both of you slowly. While in effect, you can tap the skill button again for a quick HP boost.
Kelly allows you to run faster and increase the damage of your first shot after a sprint
Kenta's skill creates a shield in front of you that reduces damage received
Kla's raw punches deal more damage
Koda can reveal enemies that are standing up, even behind covers. This info is not shared with your teammates.
Your scoped shots are more accurate with Laura's skill
Leon helps you recover from fights by restoring your HP slowly a few seconds after you're hit
Lila is a good fit for Riflers. Her passive will slow down enemies you hit with assault or marksman rifles. Knocking down the slowed enemy also gives you a Gloo Wall.
Luna makes you shoot faster. Once you hit an enemy, you'll also run faster.
Whenever you eliminate an enemy, Luqueta increases your Shield Points.
Maro is perfect for long range. You'll deal more damage the further you are from enemies.
You can eat mushrooms and use Med Kits faster with Maxim
With Miguel, you'll get a massive amount of EP after a knockdown.
Misha is perfect for those on the road. You become harder to hit and take less damage in any vehicle.
Moco will mark enemies you hit with arrows displayed above their heads, making it easier for your team to locate them.
Morse's skill lets you enter Stealth mode. While in stealth, you will not be detected, and are harder to be spotted by faraway enemies, but you can't fire either.
Morse is perfect for flankers to sneak up on enemies undetected.
If you get too close, enemies can still spot you even if you're in Stealth, so get your weapon ready.
Nairi makes your Gloo Walls sturdier and provides temporary healing
Nero can throw a plushie forward to create a dream space near it. Enemies inside the space cannot deploy Gloo Walls and take damage over time.
If enemies are far away, throw the plushie at them beforehand so they won't be able to use Gloo Walls to block your damage.
Don't worry if you miss a little, the plushie will automatically track the nearest enemy.
With Nikita, you can reload faster and the enemies you hit will receive less healing.
Notora slowly recovers you and your teammates' health while in a vehicle
Olivia lets you heal more and spread the healing to nearby teammates
Orion's active transforms you into a crimson ball for a short time using Energy Points. In this state, you are immune to damage and can absorb nearby enemies' HP. Try using it with Miguel's passive!
When your health is low, Orion's transformation lets you avoid all damage for a few seconds and gain HP by absorbing nearby enemies' health.
Use Orion's skill in close combat or if you're a Rusher. With Miguel's passive, you can transform again after knocking down an enemy.
Oscar's skill allows you to charge forward and destroy Gloo Walls, knocking back enemies in your way with extra damage.
When you knock down or get knocked down by an enemy, Otho will mark and slow them and their nearby teammates.
With Paloma, your shots become more accurate for a short while after breaking a Gloo Wall.
Rafael can make your sniper and marksman rifle shots silenced. The enemy you knock down also bleeds faster.
Rin can summon up to three kunai around her. The kunai will fly to any Gloo Wall or enemy you hit and deal damage. They are especially effective against Gloo Walls.
Ryden can release a spider in the target direction and catch the first enemy found
Deploy the spider forward to scout enemies ahead or place one behind you to watch for flanks
While the spider is moving, tap the skill again to stop it.
Santino's skill sends a mannequin forward. You can switch places with it two times within a certain distance.
Shani's passive gives you and your nearby teammates shield points whenever you use your active skill
Shirou will mark the enemy who's attacked you and share their location with your team
Skyler's active skill releases a sonic wave forward and destroys the Gloo Wall hit
Sonia's skill keeps you alive for a few seconds after taking a fatal hit. Use this chance to turn the fight around.
Steffie's skill creates a defensive area that disables any incoming throwables. It also provides you and your teammates inside with armor recovery and damage reduction.
Suzy gets you extra FF Coins after your team eliminates an enemy you marked
Tatsuya's skill gives you a quick dash forward and it resets after a knockdown
Use Tatsuya's dash to get in close or to dodge enemy attacks
When you knock an enemy down, your skill's cooldown resets immediately for you to dash again right away.
With Thiva, you'll help your downed up teammate faster. If successful, they'll also receive extra HP.
Hi there, I'm your skill assistant! Drag me to the skills you'd like to learn about!
With Wolfrahh, you'll deal more and take less headshot damage each time you eliminate an enemy.
Wukong allows you to transform into a bush, making it harder for enemies to target you.
Use Wukong's transformation to sneak up on enemies. Watch out for any moving bushes, they could be enemy Wukong!
If you knock down an enemy, you can immediately transform again.
Xayne's active grants you temporary shield points for protection. Knockdown streaks will make those extra SP last longer.
It's better to activate the skill before a fight starts. The extra temporary Shield Points will give you an advantage over your enemy.
Xayne is perfect for rushers and close combat. Knock down an enemy while the skill is active to refresh the extra Shield Points!

Nearby Player
{0}m
Search for nearby players
No online players nearby
Location access denied, please enable it in permission settings.
Please allow the app to access your location to recommend nearby players. You can manage this permission in Settings.
Feature Overview\n1. The Nearby Player Recommendation is an optional feature to enrich your gaming experience.\n2. Your location information will only be obtained and used with your explicit authorization, and it will be used to recommend nearby players to you.\n3. Declining authorization will not affect your other in-game experiences; only this feature will be unavailable.\n\nInformation Collection and Use\n1. After granting authorization, location information is only collected while you are using this feature.\n2. After you are offline, location data is automatically deleted.\n3. Your precise location information will not be displayed to other players; only an approximate distance range will be shown.\n4. You can also adjust the settings to disable the display of the approximate range or opt out of being recommended to nearby players.\n5. For the safety of your account and the game's social environment, we will also run anti-cheat detection on location info to prevent malicious players from using cheats to alter their location.\n\nAuthorization and Cancellation\n1. When using the feature for the first time, a mobile system authorization pop-up will appear. You may choose to grant or decline the authorization.\n2. You may turn off the location permission for this game anytime through your mobile device's settings.\n3. Once it's turned off, we will not collect your location information.
Privacy Policy

1. Map recommendation is upgraded to suggest better maps based on preferences and play habit.\n2. The new map recommendations for new players now feature top maps and will suggest maps based on your preferences.\n3. Craftland maps now support chat, friend list, and player detailed info checking.
1. Faster object loading when entering the editor. \n2. When selected, the objects are now highlighted with a gold outline for clearer feedback.\n3. New horse vehicle added.\n4. Objects can now hide their appearances.\n5. Collision component now supports viewing the collision shapes.
Asset Store now supports downloading and sharing block script and UI templates.
1. Added autosave feature for player component custom attributes to save data to the next match. Supports basic types and lists, such as integer lists. A single attribute save limit is 4KB. If the data length is too long, it may lead to data loss.\n2. Creator Center supports viewing and editing autosaved data. Please edit it after the player is offline, or changes will not take effect.
1. Craftland Studio layout adjusted to separate local drafts and published/online maps.\n2. Project file limit increased. Local drafts have no limit.\n\nExplore to discover other new features and stay tuned for more coming soon!

Grenade
Season Rewards
Claimed
BR-RANKED
There are 6 divisions (Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, Heroic) in Ranked, each with some sub-divisions for you to progress. In Battle Royale, achievements such as getting high placing in matches, eliminating enemies, helping up teammates, dealing damage will all increase the RP you get from each match. In Clash Squad, progression is only awarded to match winners.
When your RP reaches a certain level, you will be promoted to the next division. Likewise, if your RP drops to a certain level, you will be demoted to a lower division.
As you progress along each division, you will receive rewards (one-time only). When the season ends, season rewards will be given according to the highest division you achieved in the entire season.
Ranked leaderboards are sorted by division and sub-divisions. You can see how you place amongst your friends. If your rank is high enough, you may even find yourself in the global leaderboards.
At the end of each season, rank will be reset. Depending on your performance in the previous season, you might begin at a higher rank when the new season begins.

Find the corresponding ammo for your weapon of choice.
Vests and helmets reduce bullet damage and are automatically equipped.
Can't carry any more? Get rid of the items you don't need before looting further.
The red area indicates that an airstrike will be happening over that area soon. Hide inside buildings to remain safe.
Jumping off a bridge is most likely not a wise choice.
Communicate with your teammates over voice chat to improve your odds of survival.
Avoiding open areas is critical, especially when the Safe Zone is small.
Crouching and going prone reduce the chances of an enemy spotting you.
There is a daily bonus of 50 gold coins if you survive long enough!
There is a daily bonus of  when you complete your first match of the day.
Why not stay in the game and check out how other players enjoy the game?
Make use of terrain and obstacles as cover.

You may report a user while spectating.

Stick together, team! Landing at the same area allows your teammates to cover you in need.
Areas known to have good loot will always attract more players. High risk, high return!
Skydiving lets you descent quicker, air surfing lets you surf farther.
Sprinting increases your movement speed, but you won't be able to fire and sprint at the same time.
You will not be able to shoot when swimming in the water, duh!
Gather up some friends and try to survive as a team!

Winning teams share loot with teammates.
Normal tyres boost your jump, fixed tyres propel you to a fixed spot.
Vehicles are fast, but don't expect to surprise your enemies with the noise you are creating.

Congratulations, you joined a guild!
1. Level up guilds by accumulating Glory. The higher the level, the more privileges the whole guild can enjoy.\n2. Guild privileges include: additional gold and experience,  daily gold limit increased and guild member limit increased.\n3. Players can gain glory by playing games or through diamond top-up:\na. Glory gained after each game will depend on your placing in the match;\nb. Playing games with your guild mates, will get you 4 glory, with a daily cap of 80;\nc. For every 10 diamonds purchased, you will get 1 glory.\n4. When a player leaves a guild, his glory will be reset.\n5. The amount of glory that a guild can get in a day is capped, proportionate to the maximum number of members the guild can have. When the daily cap is reached, the guild will no longer receive any additional glory for the day.
1. Guild size is limited to 20 people at first. This limit can be increased by leveling up the guild or purchasing a guild expansion. \n2. Every guild can have 1 leader and 3 elders.\n3. Guild leaders can: appoint or demote elders, approve guild requests, kick members, change the slogan and announcement, change the guild name and expand the guild. Elders can: approve guild requests, kick members, change the slogan and announcement and expand the guild.\n4. Leaders and elders can invite their friends and other players into the guild, with a maximum of 30 invites. Players can invite their guild mates to a squad.\n5. Players may not leave a guild within 24 hours of joining. \n6. When Guild Leader has been offline for longer than 30 days, to keep the Guild running, members who are in this guild for longer than 7 days may apply to become Acting Guild Leader. Position is effective upon application. If Acting Guild Leader is offline for longer than 7 days, his/her position is automatically invalidated.

Set the global sleep threshold, which is only valid for newly generated Dynamic rigid bodies.
Sleep threshold. When the movement and rotation of a rigid body are less than this value for a period of time, it will enter a sleep state. The rigid body in sleep state will no longer perform physical calculations.
Get the global sleep threshold
The sleep threshold is 0.005 by default. When the rigid body's movement and rotation are less than this value for a period of time, it will enter the sleep state. The rigid body in the sleep state will no longer perform physical calculations.

Base coefficient, multiplied by the original value before all calculations are performed.
Pre-accumulator, accumulates before multiplication, participates in multiplication calculations
Consecutive multipliers, for example (reduced to 90%, strengthened to 110%) have the effect of Value*90%*110%.
The effect of a multiplication accumulator, e.g. (add 10%, add 5%) is Value*(1+10%+5%)
Post accumulator, not involved in multiplication calculations, stacked at the end
